page.title: How Much Does Medicare Cost in {year}? Premiums, Deductibles & Help | Vernal Medicare
page.heading: How Much Does Medicare Cost in {year}?
page.description: A plain-English guide to Medicare costs in {year}: Part A, B, and D premiums and deductibles, the IRMAA income surcharge, out-of-pocket limits, penalties, and the programs that lower your bill.
page.summary: Medicare in {year} has a few moving parts: the standard Part B premium is {partBPremium}/month with a {partBDeductible} deductible, Part A has a {partADeductible} hospital deductible, and Part D drug costs are capped at {partDCap} out of pocket. Higher earners pay an IRMAA surcharge; lower earners may pay far less through assistance programs. This hub walks through each piece and points you to the calculator for your own number.

faqs.question: What is the standard Medicare cost in {year}?
faqs.answer: Most people pay the standard Part B premium of {partBPremium}/month (Part A is usually premium-free if you worked 10+ years). On top of that you may have a Part D drug plan and, if you choose one, a Medigap or Medicare Advantage premium. Deductibles: {partADeductible} for Part A hospital stays and {partBDeductible} for Part B.

faqs.question: Why is my Medicare more expensive than my neighbor’s?
faqs.answer: Usually income. Higher earners pay an IRMAA surcharge on Part B and Part D based on their income from two years ago. Two people with the same coverage can pay very different premiums purely because of IRMAA.

faqs.question: Can I lower what I pay for Medicare?
faqs.answer: Often, yes. Lower-income beneficiaries may qualify for Extra Help or a Medicare Savings Program that cuts premiums and drug costs. Higher earners can plan around IRMAA brackets. Choosing the right drug and supplement plan also matters — that’s where a local agent helps.

nextSteps.action: run the Medicare cost estimator, then call us free to compare plans line by line.

meta.ogTitle: How Much Does Medicare Cost in {year}?
meta.ogDescription: Premiums, deductibles, IRMAA, out-of-pocket limits, and the programs that lower your bill.

body.introHtml: "How much does Medicare cost?" doesn't have one answer — it depends on your parts, your income, and your plan choices. This page is the map. Below, each piece of your Medicare bill in {year}, with links to go deeper or run your own numbers.
blockItemsHtml: Part A (hospital) — usually premium-free if you or a spouse worked 10+ years. The {year} hospital deductible is {partADeductible} per benefit period.
Part B (medical) — a standard premium of {partBPremium}/month and a {partBDeductible} annual deductible. See how it fits with Part A in Part A vs Part B.
Part D (drugs) — a plan premium (the {year} base is about {partDBase}) plus copays, with out-of-pocket drug costs now capped at {partDCap} a year and insulin at {insulinCap}/month.
Medigap or Medicare Advantage — the coverage you add on top. Compare in our cost estimator and Supplement vs Advantage guide.
body.estimateHtml: Numbers on a page only go so far. The Medicare Cost Estimator adds up premiums, deductibles, and expected out-of-pocket for a Medigap path versus a Medicare Advantage path, so you can compare real totals for your situation.
body.h2Irmaa: If Your Income Is Higher: IRMAA
body.irmaaHtml: Above {irmaaSingle} in income (single) or {irmaaMarried} (married), you pay an income surcharge called IRMAA on Part B and Part D. Look up the exact amount in the {year} IRMAA brackets, estimate yours, and if you're over a line, learn how to reduce it legally or appeal after an income drop.
body.h2Assistance: If Your Income Is Lower: Assistance
body.assistanceHtml: You may pay far less than the standard amounts. Start at the help-paying-for-Medicare hub — it sorts every program by income — or check the {year} income limits directly. Extra Help lowers Part D drug costs, and the Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI) can pay your Part B premium outright. Struggling with drug costs specifically? See prescription drug assistance.
capItemsHtml: Out-of-pocket maximum — how Medicare Advantage caps your yearly exposure (Original Medicare alone has no cap).
Late-enrollment penalties — a permanent surcharge for signing up late; estimate yours.
High-Deductible Plan G — a lower-premium way to buy Medigap protection.
body.localHtml: Plan premiums and availability are local. See what Medicare costs in Uintah County for the plans actually offered here.
body.outroHtml: Medicare's pieces add up differently for everyone. The fastest way to your real number is a quick conversation — call us free and we'll price it out with you, no obligation.
